props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent crude traded above $90 overnight on confirmed strikes before easing toward the high $88s on a reported ceasefire proposal, while WTI returned to the low $82s.
The semiconductor index fell into correction from its June high, emerging as the market’s weak point into megacap earnings week.
The US 10-year Treasury yield sat near 4.55 percent.
The UK 10-year gilt was near 4.98 percent and sterling firmed as Andy Burnham took office.

items.what: FIFA decides whether to open a formal disciplinary charge over the Malvinas banner.
SpaceX targets Starship Flight 13 after a scrub.
Moonshot releases full weights for Kimi K3.
Megacap earnings arrive from Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM.
The WHO issues its next update on the DRC Ebola death toll.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-20 · Sprockett, Tinkerton, Cogsworth, Graves, Foreman · three of the Colosseum arc’s calls settled with the final

open_calls.question: The United States and Iran announce a ceasefire or a formal pause in strikes
open_calls.detail: A 10-day proposal reached traders and moved oil, but no government has confirmed it and Central Command reported a ninth consecutive night of strikes; a pause within the week is possible but not yet the base case.

open_calls.question: Front-month Brent crude settles at or above $90 a barrel on a trading day this week
open_calls.detail: Brent topped $90 overnight before easing to the high $88s as de-escalation talk erased part of the premium; a fresh settle above $90 needs another leg the strikes could still supply.

open_calls.question: FIFA opens a formal disciplinary charge against the Argentine federation over the Malvinas banner
open_calls.detail: FIFA has only been assessing match reports since the England semifinal; a comparable 2014 penalty landed after that tournament, so a formal charge this week is less likely than one later.

open_calls.question: Moonshot releases the full Kimi K3 model weights
open_calls.detail: Moonshot has publicly committed to a 27 July weights release and staged the launch as an operations problem; the main risk is another compute-capacity slip, not a change of intent.

open_calls.question: SpaceX Starship Flight 13 lifts off
open_calls.detail: The attempt slipped to 23 July after a last-second abort on 16 July; the vehicle is staged and the window is set, but a further scrub is a live possibility on a test article.

open_calls.question: The next WHO update shows the DRC Bundibugyo Ebola death toll above 900
open_calls.detail: WHO logged 828 deaths as of mid-July with the outbreak still spreading and local ministry tallies already higher; the trajectory points above 900 by the next bulletin absent a sharp containment turn.

open_calls.question: Federal authorities publish an after-action accounting of enforcement at tournament venues
open_calls.detail: The arc’s standing discriminator on the enforcement shadow: a documented federal accounting by mid-August gives the shadow shape; agencies rarely publish venue-level enforcement data on that timeline.

items.what: Opponents held 142 protests against data centres across 42 states, organised by HumansFirst.
Hyundai is moving to buy out SoftBank’s remaining stake in Boston Dynamics.
The ECB business survey showed selling-price expectations easing near 3.2 percent.
Jonas Vingegaard abandoned the Tour de France after a crash and collarbone fracture, reshaping the race.
